expectations.text: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
expectations.evidence: Separate version availability checks for Rapid and Stable channels, separate post-upgrade sections per cluster (Dev 1/2 Rapid, Prod 1/2 Stable), and timeline notes differentiating dev vs prod.

expectations.text: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Key Differences for Autopilot Clusters' section: 'Google manages node pool upgrades and surge settings, OS patching automatic, Control plane upgrades scheduled during maintenance windows.'

expectations.text: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
expectations.evidence: Pre-upgrade checklist includes 'No deprecated API usage across all clusters' with command to check GKE deprecation insights dashboard.

expectations.text: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
expectations.evidence: Explicit item: 'All containers have resource requests AND limits defined' with note 'Autopilot requires requests on all containers; pods without requests will fail to schedule.'

expectations.text: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
expectations.evidence: Workload Readiness section covers PDBs, bare pods, terminationGracePeriodSeconds, StatefulSet backups, and PV reclaim policies.

expectations.text: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
expectations.evidence: Per-cluster post-upgrade checks include 'Control plane at 1.30' and 'All nodes at 1.30' with gcloud commands.

expectations.text: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
expectations.evidence: Workload Health section covers deployments, CrashLoopBackOff, Pending pods, StatefulSets, PDBs, and ingress. Application Validation section adds smoke tests.

expectations.text: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
expectations.evidence: Timeline Notes section establishes dev (Rapid) gets 1.30 first, prod (Stable) follows after validation. Structure lists dev clusters first, implying test-on-dev-first workflow. Pre-freeze checklist timing also suggests this.
Observability section in post-upgrade covers metrics pipeline, log aggregation, error rates, latency baselines, and new error patterns.
